Description: Merged with Denver-based IWP Family Office to form a ~$10B+ AUA multi-family office of 90+ professionals across St. Louis, Denver, and Dallas-Fort Worth, ~140 UHNW families.
BW Forsyth Partners provided strategic evergreen capital backing the merger and future acquisitions; Katherine Lintz managing partner, IWP's Charlie Willhoit CEO.
Portfolio manager Dylan C. Brix, CFA, CTFA was promoted to Partner (joined 2020).
Wellspring Financial Advisors rebranded as Wellspring Family Office to reflect its multigenerational-family model (~$6.5B client net worth).
The Pritzker Organization took a minority equity stake in a long-term strategic partnership; some Pritzker family members became clients.
Opened a new ~$793K position in the Dimensional Short-Duration Fixed Income ETF (DFSD) in its latest 13F.
Andreas Arni (ex-Lombard Odier/Credit Suisse) took over as CEO and Managing Partner as long-serving CEO Martin Sutter moved to the board.
Flagship pooled fund LMI Partners LLC reported cumulative capital raised rising ~$1.05B to ~$1.26B (~$210M net new inflows) in its latest SEC Form D/A.
Two TFO leaders earned dual recognition on AdvisorHub's 2026 '1000 Advisors to Watch' list (Over $1B category).
Financial Advisor Magazine's 2025 RIA Survey ranked TFO the #2 largest RIA headquartered in Arizona.
CEO David Savir and SVP Kristopher Martin appeared on the Family Business Magazine Podcast discussing family office structures.
Affiliated investment bank Class VI Partners represented Helimix in its acquisition by YETI, part of a 2025-2026 exit/M&A pipeline that channels newly-liquid business owners into the family office.
Class VI Partners advised Electric Equipment & Engineering on its recapitalization by HKW Inc., one of several 2025 liquidity events from the affiliated deal practice.
Regulatory assets under management jumped ~185% year-over-year to $14.4B, per its latest Form ADV.
Named to AdvisorHub's inaugural '50 RIA Firms to Watch' 2026 list.
Newly formed Plano firm registered with the SEC as an independent family office managing ~$144M.
CEO Kathleen Grace named to Forbes/SHOOK Top Women Wealth Advisors Best-In-State list for 2026.
Boca Raton RIA led by ex-Goldman Sachs exec Kathleen Grace announced plans to acquire small women-led wealth practices from retiring female executives in South Florida.
MFO officially launched, founded by Andrew Crofton and Scott Essex with a six-person owner-partner team; offices in New York, Chicago, West Palm Beach, San Francisco.
Registered with the SEC (2025-04-28), formalizing its transition from the Ballast Point principals' single-family office into a registered multi-family office serving select outside families.
Dan White launched Sestante as Co-Founder/Managing Partner/CCO in early 2025, moving over from CIO of affiliated Sestante Capital Advisors.
Tom Ruggie named to the InvestmentNews 2026 Top Financial Professionals list (100 leading US advisors).
Founder/CEO Tom Ruggie named an InvestmentNews Advisor of the Year awardee for Alternative Investments.
Launched as a newly SEC-registered RIA (Georgia entity formed 2025-08-20) under CEO Michael Callahan, serving families below single-family-office scale (~$189M AUM).
Amended its Form D offering to $6,500,000 combined equity and debt as it scales its private-markets platform.
Filed a Reg D (Form D) raise of $1,000,000 in debt, signaling early capital formation shortly after launch.
Launched as a newly SEC-registered boutique multi-family office (~$327M AUM), $10M account minimum.
